summary: We recommend seeding D2 with 20 hand-crafted pairs that each target one specific failure mode of the HeuristicVerifier rather than synthesizing random low-quality pairs because targeted fixtures let D3 attribute confusion matrix entries to a named rubric axis. The tradeoff against using real-world low-quality PRs is that crafted pairs cannot capture the long tail of stylistic badness; this is acceptable for the first pass because the goal of D3 is to validate that each rubric dimension actually fires in isolation. All 20 pairs were verified to score strictly below the canonical floor of 3.5 and a MANIFEST md records each pair's slug overall score and the dimension it targets.

claims.statement: 3 clarity pairs a1 a2 a3 score 3.4 2.8 and 2.8 because the python clarity dimension scores 5 for 2-4 sentence summaries 3 for 1 or 5 sentence summaries and 2 for everything else which a1 with 1 sentence a2 with 6 sentences and a3 with 10 sentences each exercise distinctly.

citations.title: HeuristicVerifier _score_clarity uses 2-4 sentence band as 5 and 1 or 5 as 3 and otherwise 2
MANIFEST md records each a-prefixed pair with its overall score against the verifier

claims.caveats: The clarity dimension splits sentences on period and exclamation mark; future pairs that use unicode sentence terminators may not split as expected which is an enhancement for a later D2 pass.

claims.statement: 4 citation_quality pairs b1 b2 b3 b4 score 2.8 2.8 3.0 and 2.2 because citation_quality scores 5 for at least half primary 4 for at least a quarter primary 3 for at least one primary 2 for secondary only and 1 for community-only or no citations which b1 b2 b3 b4 each exercise distinctly.

citations.title: HeuristicVerifier _score_citation_quality uses the primary share thresholds 0.5 0.25 1 primary present 1 secondary present and otherwise community-only
MANIFEST md records each b-prefixed pair with its sourcing profile and overall score

claims.caveats: The spec model rejects high-confidence claims with zero citations at parse time so b1 uses a low-confidence claim with empty citations which lets the report parse and citation_quality return 1; if D3 wants to also fixture the parse-rejection path that is a separate failure class outside the verdict score and would need its own bucket.

claims.statement: the remaining 13 pairs covering confidence_calibration coverage decision_usefulness and combined pathological all score strictly below 3.5 ranging from 1.4 to 3.4 and the lowest scoring pair f4-combined-worst at 1.4 exercises clarity citation_quality and confidence_calibration simultaneously while the highest-scoring failing pair a1 at 3.4 still fails the gate because the floor is strictly greater than or equal to 3.5.

citations.title: MANIFEST md records all 20 pair scores ranging 1.4 to 3.4
open_outcome cli verify exits non-zero when overall is strictly less than the floor argument

claims.caveats: Pairs scoring 3.4 and 3.2 are close to the floor; if D5 ratchets the floor up to 4.0 these pairs remain failing but pairs near 3.5 in the passing set may flip to failing which is intentional behavior of the ratchet not a fixture problem.

open_questions: Should D2 also include a small set of pairs that fail at schema parse rather than at score so D3 can report parse-failure rate alongside the confusion matrix?
methodology_notes: Inspected sdk-python open_outcome verifier py to enumerate the exact thresholds for each dimension then authored 20 pairs each targeting one threshold. After an initial seed 6 pairs scored at or above 3.5; each was rewritten to push one or more additional dimensions to the floor until all 20 scored below 3.5. Final scores were recorded in MANIFEST md.
